#  Filter Drawing Markups 
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Read Only' or above permissions on the Drawings tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Filters will be listed regardless of whether the markup is present on the drawing or not.
    * Filters will only be available if the tool is enabled on the project AND the tool is enabled as a markup on the drawings tool. (e.g. If the project does not have the Observations tool enabled, the observations filter will not appear.)


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Drawings** tool.
  2. Click **Number** next to a drawing.
  3. Click the **Filter** icon.
  4. Click the **Eye** icon next to a layer or markup type to show or hide the layer or markup type.


_Note:_
  * A **blue eye** icon indicates the layer or markup type is visible, while a **gray eye** icon indicates it is hidden. In the example below, both the Published and Personal layers are visible, but markup types such as RFIs and Submittals are currently hidden.
  * Click **Correspondence** to see a list of correspondence types and to show or hide the markup for each type.![Filter Drawing Layers](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/cec7cd3b641821ef457547b80df0927f/c5a9c227f938f91343ff1f69d289281d/filter-drawing-layers.png?fm=webp)


## See Also

  * [Open a Drawing](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/open-a-drawing)
  * [Drawings - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/permissions)
title: Filter Drawing Markups
description: How to filter drawing markups that appear in your drawings.
